item.text: The prompt-exclusion fix handles the empty/single-line prompt case. I’m checking the existing multiline chat-buffer path because this UI already supports chat prompt line breaks, and that affects whether the prompt is always the trailing `chat> ...` line.

There is a remaining edge case in the prompt stripping logic: the chat buffer can contain embedded newlines, so the appended input prompt is no longer just the final line. I’m checking whether tests cover that path before I decide the final finding.

item.aggregated_output:
rg: the literal "\n" is not allowed in a regex

Consider enabling multiline mode with the --multiline flag (or -U for short).
When multiline mode is enabled, new line characters can be matched.

item.text: The existing tests cover keeping the prompt visible and the new empty-buffer right-pane yank, but I don’t see coverage for visual selection while the chat draft is multiline. That path matters because the app explicitly supports chat prompt line breaks.

item.aggregated_output:
276	
   277	`src/terminal_app.rs::TerminalApp<B>` translates raw terminal bytes and modal editing state into
   278	direct `WorkLeafController<B>` calls for in-process use. `src/terminal_app.rs::RemoteTerminalApp`
   279	uses the same terminal state machine with `HttpControllerClient` for the CLI/daemon process split.
   280	Both adapters keep a local render snapshot, apply `WorkLeafEvent` values to that cache and to
   281	`TerminalUi`, and render from the cache rather than fetching a full controller snapshot for every
   282	frame. They own terminal event-loop concerns such as insert mode, prompt mode, `Ctrl-W` navigation,
   283	SGR mouse clicks, SGR mouse wheel scrolling of the right pane, chunked terminal input parsing,
   284	rendering invalidation, and polling background workers. Insert mode sends chat text to the selected
   285	agent session, or to `command-agent` when the Work Leaf command surface is selected. Bracketed-paste
   286	newlines and Shift+Enter are chat prompt line breaks. A plain Enter submits the buffered chat text.
   287	When an agent chat is selected in command mode, `/` focuses the chat, seeds the chat buffer with
   288	`/`, and enters insert mode so `/status`-style input submits through the same selected-agent chat
   289	path.
   290	Command mode supports visual text selection in the focused pane: `v` starts character selection,
   291	`V` starts line selection, `Ctrl-V` starts block selection, `h/j/k/l` and arrow keys move the
   292	selection cursor, and `y` or `Y` yank selected text. Yanked text is stored in terminal UI state and
   293	emitted through an OSC 52 terminal clipboard sequence on the next rendered frame.

item.text:
FINDINGS

- `src/ui.rs:1645` only removes the right-pane prompt when the final cached line starts with `chat> `. But `src/terminal_app.rs:549` allows multiline chat drafts, and `src/cli.rs:1199` renders those as `chat> first line\nsecond line`; in that case the final line no longer starts with `chat> `, so `pane_line_texts(PaneFocus::Right)` at `src/ui.rs:1237` includes the chat input draft as selectable/yankable text. A right-pane visual yank with a multiline draft can still copy prompt/input text instead of transcript/output, and the new regression test only covers the empty single-line prompt case.
